title: Today's AI Brief: Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including Cognition's $1 billion raise and the challenges of using AI in journalism.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans
imageCaption: Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans
imageSourceTitle: Cognition’s Scott Wu says AI coding agents shouldn’t replace humans

body.text: I'm looking at a couple of interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First, Cognition, a two-year-old AI coding agent startup, has raised $1 billion at a whopping $26 billion valuation. Their product, Devin, is one of the first AI coding agents on the market. What's notable here is that Cognition's CEO, Scott Wu, is saying that AI coding agents shouldn't replace humans. This raises questions about the role of AI in the workforce and how it's changing the way we work.

According to TechCrunch, Wu's comments suggest that AI coding agents are meant to augment human capabilities, not replace them. This is an interesting perspective, especially given the rapid advancements in AI technology. It seems that Cognition is positioning itself as a leader in this space, and their recent funding round suggests that investors are excited about their potential.

In a separate story, WIRED had an... interesting experience using AI to interview the author of a book about the impact of AI on truth. The interview didn't quite go as planned, highlighting some of the challenges of using AI in journalism. It's clear that AI is still a work in progress, and there are many kinks to work out before it can be used effectively in situations like this.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
